fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: If not set, a random client ID is used.

fields.label: Use TLS Client Auth
fields.description: Enables TLS authentication using client cert configured in secure json data.

fields.label: Skip TLS Verification
fields.description: When enabled, skips verification of the MQTT server's TLS certificate chain and host name.

fields.label: With CA Cert
fields.description: Needed for verifying servers with self-signed TLS Certs.

fields.label: TLS CA Certificate
fields.description: If a Certificate Authority certificate is required to verify the server's certificate, provide it here.

fields.label: TLS Client Certificate
fields.description: To authenticate with an TLS client certificate, provide the client certificate here.

fields.label: TLS Client Key
fields.description: To authenticate with a client TLS certificate, provide the private key here.

relationships.description: Optional MQTT basic-auth username/password pair. Passed to paho.SetUsername / paho.SetPassword when non-empty (pkg/mqtt/client.go:54-60).
TLS client key pair loaded via tls.X509KeyPair when either value is non-empty (pkg/mqtt/client.go:66-73). Both must be provided together — providing only one causes the handshake to fail.

Editor gating: the tlsAuth switch controls whether the TLS Client Certificate / TLS Client Key inputs are rendered (src/ConfigEditor.tsx:107-122). The backend itself does not read tlsAuth — it uses whichever of tlsClientCert/tlsClientKey are present (pkg/mqtt/client.go:66-73).

Editor gating: the tlsAuthWithCACert switch controls whether the TLS CA Certificate input is rendered (src/ConfigEditor.tsx:107-122). The backend itself does not read tlsAuthWithCACert — it uses tlsCACert whenever it is non-empty (pkg/mqtt/client.go:75-79).

instructions.msg: The MQTT datasource uses no explicit auth discriminator. Authentication is composed from any combination of: (a) MQTT basic auth via jsonData.username + secureJsonData.password (paho.SetUsername / paho.SetPassword only fire when the values are non-empty — pkg/mqtt/client.go:54-60), and (b) TLS client authentication via secureJsonData.tlsClientCert + secureJsonData.tlsClientKey (loaded via tls.X509KeyPair when either is non-empty — pkg/mqtt/client.go:66-73). Both can be combined with a custom CA in secureJsonData.tlsCACert (pkg/mqtt/client.go:75-79). Choose auth by which secrets you set; jsonData.tlsAuth and jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert are editor-visibility toggles only and are not read by the backend.

Minimal working payload — anonymous broker: {"jsonData":{"uri":"tcp://broker.example.com:1883"}}. Only jsonData.uri is required — the backend calls opts.AddBroker(o.URI) unconditionally with no fallback (pkg/mqtt/client.go:46) and a missing URI produces a connection error against an empty broker address. If jsonData.clientID is empty the backend generates a random client id of the form 'grafana_<int>' (pkg/mqtt/client.go:48-52).

Basic auth payload: {"jsonData":{"uri":"tcp://broker.example.com:1883","username":"user"},"secureJsonData":{"password":"<password>"}}. TLS client auth payload: {"jsonData":{"uri":"tls://broker.example.com:8883","tlsAuth":true},"secureJsonData":{"tlsClientCert":"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----...","tlsClientKey":"-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----..."}}. Self-signed server + client-cert: add "tlsAuthWithCACert":true and "tlsCACert":"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----...". tlsClientCert and tlsClientKey MUST be provided together — pkg/mqtt/client.go:66-73 calls tls.X509KeyPair when either is non-empty, and the call fails if only one side is present.

secureJsonData values (password, tlsCACert, tlsClientCert, tlsClientKey) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.tlsClientKey == true) instead of secureJsonData. The password reset button explicitly clears the stored value via updateDatasourcePluginResetOption (src/ConfigEditor.tsx:21-23); the TLS certificate reset buttons behave the same way (src/TLSConfig.tsx:51-53,80-82,107-109).

jsonData.uri is a paho broker URI, not an HTTP URL. Valid schemes are tcp://, tls:// (or ssl://, tcps://, mqtts:// — all treated as MQTT-over-TLS), ws://, and wss:// (pkg/mqtt/proxy.go:69-80 default-port mapping). Include the host and port explicitly (e.g. tcp://broker:1883, tls://broker:8883, ws://broker:80, wss://broker:443) — omitting the port makes the paho library apply the scheme's default. jsonData.tlsSkipVerify=true disables server certificate verification for tls:// / wss:// connections (pkg/mqtt/client.go:62-64); use only with trusted infrastructure.

Editor-only toggles: jsonData.tlsAuth and jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert exist only to gate the visibility of the TLS Configuration section in the editor (src/ConfigEditor.tsx:107-122). The Go backend's mqtt.Options struct (pkg/mqtt/client.go:26-35) does not include either field — it decides TLS behavior purely by whether the corresponding secrets are non-empty. Setting a toggle without the matching secret has no runtime effect; setting a secret without the toggle still works because the backend never reads the toggles.
